commands.repair_reason: The PR appears valid but needs human maintainer handling for mergeability, review, and broad validation rather than an automated repair PR.; Cleared: The diff touches authorization-sensitive owner-only tools, but retained tools fail closed on execution and no dependency, CI, secret, or supply-chain surface is changed. (sha=484cf48b974e554f18d0562d179823d9e682404c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR gates Codex native PreToolUse hook relay registration on actual before-tool policy and retains owner-only dynamic tool schemas with fail-closed execution guards to avoid Codex app-server thread churn.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main unconditionally enables pre_tool_use relay work and filters owner-only tools out of non-owner dynamic schemas, while the linked reports describe the observed process fanout and thread churn. I did not rerun a live reproduction in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: The PR appears valid but needs human maintainer handling for mergeability, review, and broad validation rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex keeps owner-only dynamic tool schemas stable across sender-owner flips, so app-server threads resume instead of constantly starting fresh native Codex sessions.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. That meant default Codex app-server config could still register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools were filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change could alter the registered dynamic tool names and force a `thread/start` instead of `thread/resume`. Since Codex receives dynamic tool schemas on thread start, not as a cheap per-turn update, this made a transient availability flip expensive and session-churning.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now only reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` opts Codex dynamic tool construction into stable owner-only schema registration.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` can retain unavailable owner-only tools while wrapping their execution with a fail-closed authorization guard.
- `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` passes the new retain option through to the owner-only policy layer.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching from an owner sender to a non-owner sender could change dynamic tool registration and force a new Codex native thread.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Codex dynamic tool schemas stay stable across owner-only availability flips, but unauthorized owner-only tool execution still throws `Tool restricted to owner senders.`
- Owner to non-owner turns reuse the same Codex thread via `thread/resume`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Specifically: default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not force a fresh Codex native thread while unauthorized owner-only execution still fails closed.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `484cf48b97`, Node `v22.22.0`, Codex CLI logged in with ChatGPT auth, OpenClaw bundled `codex` plugin loaded from this branch. Private home/workspace paths and auth details redacted below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns on the same session, then inspected the redacted trajectory/sidecar, and ran a throwaway production-code runtime probe with `node --import tsx` to print the hook config, owner-only tool policy result, and thread lifecycle request sequence.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[plugins] loaded 1 plugin(s) (1 attempted) in 210.5ms
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"

$ jq -r 'select(.type=="session.started" or .type=="prompt.submitted" or .type=="model.completed" or .type=="session.ended") | [.type, .ts, (.data.threadId // ""), (.data.turnId // ""), (.data.assistantTexts[0] // .data.status // "")] | @tsv' <redacted-session>.trajectory.jsonl
session.started  2026-05-18T01:37:55.836Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39
prompt.submitted 2026-05-18T01:37:55.843Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bb-8c82-7ff1-91cd-3723111902c0
model.completed  2026-05-18T01:38:02.058Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bb-8c82-7ff1-91cd-3723111902c0  OK
session.ended    2026-05-18T01:38:02.058Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bb-8c82-7ff1-91cd-3723111902c0  success
session.started  2026-05-18T01:39:54.972Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39
prompt.submitted 2026-05-18T01:39:54.979Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bd-5de2-7e50-9aba-2f20ba14e6bd
model.completed  2026-05-18T01:39:58.235Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bd-5de2-7e50-9aba-2f20ba14e6bd  OK2
session.ended    2026-05-18T01:39:58.235Z  019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39  019e38bd-5de2-7e50-9aba-2f20ba14e6bd  success

$ jq '{threadId, model, modelProvider, createdAt, updatedAt, dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent:(.dynamicToolsFingerprint!=null), sessionFile:"<redacted-session-file>"}' <redacted-session>.jsonl.codex-app-server.json
{
  "threadId": "019e38bb-8c64-74a0-b5f5-fbc99f141e39",
  "model": "gpt-5.4",
  "modelProvider": null,
  "createdAt": "2026-05-18T01:37:55.835Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-18T01:39:54.970Z",
  "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true,
  "sessionFile": "<redacted-session-file>"
}
```

Production-code runtime probe output:

```json
{
  "hookRelay": {
    "preToolUse": [],
    "postToolUse": [],
    "stop": [],
    "permissionRequestCommandIncludes": {
      "permission_request": true,
      "pre_tool_use": false
    }
  },
  "ownerOnlyTools": {
    "retainedForOwner": [
      "cron",
      "gateway",
      "nodes"
    ],
    "retainedForNonOwner": [
      "cron",
      "gateway",
      "nodes"
    ],
    "sameRegisteredToolNames": true,
    "unauthorizedCronResult": "Tool restricted to owner senders."
  },
  "threadLifecycle": {
    "requestMethods": [
      "thread/start",
      "thread/resume"
    ],
    "threadIds": [
      null,
      "thread-proof-1"
    ],
    "ownerToolsInDynamicSpecs": [
      [
        "cron",
        "gateway",
        "nodes"
      ],
      []
    ],
    "preToolUseConfig": [
      [],
      []
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real Codex app-server OpenClaw session completed two live `openai-codex/gpt-5.4` turns with the same native thread id. The runtime probe shows default hook config sends `hooks.PreToolUse: []` while keeping `PermissionRequest` relay active, retained owner-only tool names are stable for owner and non-owner policy inputs, unauthorized owner-only execution throws `Tool restricted to owner senders.`, and lifecycle calls are `thread/start` followed by `thread/resume`.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip was exercised through the production owner-only policy and Codex thread lifecycle code path in the runtime probe above, and the local live Codex session verified the actual app-server harness/auth path.

## Tests / Proof

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are process/session churn in the Codex runtime path. I included the terminal proof below so reviewers can see exactly what was exercised.

Targeted formatter and whitespace checks:

```text
$ git diff --check
# no output

$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 3254ms on 9 files using 1 threads.
```

Targeted regression tests:

```text
$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  62 passed (62)
   Start at  09:05:01
   Duration  2.54s (transform 861ms, setup 129ms, import 1.98s, tests 332ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  189 passed (189)
   Start at  09:05:06
   Duration  27.56s (transform 1.83s, setup 114ms, import 4.01s, tests 23.33s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 35.38s
```

Specific regressions covered:

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: default Codex thread config has `hooks.PreToolUse: []`, command approvals still carry the `nativeHookRelay` bridge, unavailable owner-only tools are retained for Codex fingerprints, and an owner/non-owner flip produces `thread/start` then `thread/resume`.
- `src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts`: retained owner-only tools remain registered for schema stability but reject execution for unauthorized senders.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment; the focused affected suites above are green.
